title: These Two Defense Titans Will Battle to Build the Nation's Next Fighter Jet
text: Northrop Grumman will not participate in the Air Force's Next Generation Air Dominance (NGAD) competition, leaving the award to Lockheed Martin or Boeing. Lockheed has the incumbent's advantage, but for Boeing, the competition represents a chance for a fresh start.

title: Northrop Grumman Corporation (NOC) Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE:NOC ) Q2 2023 Earnings Conference Call July 27, 2023 9:00 AM ET Company Participants Todd Ernst - Corporate Vice President, Treasurer & Vice President, Investor Relations Kathy Warden - Chairman, Chief Executive Officer & President David Keffer - Corporate Vice President & Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Robert Stallard - Vertical Research Richard Safran - Seaport Global Partners Ron Epstein - Bank of America Merrill Lynch Kristine Liwag - Morgan Stanley Doug Harned - Bernstein Sheila Kahyaoglu - Jefferies Matt Akers - Wells Fargo Peter Arment - Baird George Shapiro - Shapiro Research Seth Seifman - JPMorgan Myles Walton - Wolfe Research David Strauss - Barclays Jason Gursky - Citigroup Good day ladies and gentlemen and welcome to Northrop Grumman's Second Quarter Conference Call. Today's call is being recorded.

title: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Beats on Q2 Earnings, Ups '23 Sales View
text: Northrop Grumman's (NOC) Q2 sales of $9.58 billion beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 2.7%. The top line also increases 9% from $8.80 billion in the year-ago quarter.

title: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Reports Q2 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Northrop Grumman (NOC) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Top Estimates
text: Northrop Grumman (NOC) came out with quarterly earnings of $5.34 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $5.31 per share. This compares to earnings of $6.06 per share a year ago.

title: Northrop Grumman lifts annual forecasts on weapons demand
text: Northrop Grumman raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts on Thursday, as the aerospace and defense major bets on strong weapon demand amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

title: Northrop Grumman profit falls 14% but beats target and lifts outlook
text: Northrop Grumman Corp. NOC, -0.01% stock was up by 0.8% in premarket trades Thursday after the defense contractor beat its revenue and earnings target and raised the low end of its 2023 outlook. Northrop reported second-quarter profit of $812 million, or $5.34 a share, down 14% from $946 million, or $6.06 a share, in the year-ago quarter.

title: Northrop Grumman Releases Second Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: FALLS CHURCH, Va., July 27, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) has released its second quarter 2023 financial results. A copy of the earnings release has been furnished in the company's Form 8-K filing and is also available on the company's investor relations website at http://investor.northropgrumman.com.

title: Investing In UAP Disclosure, And How To Manage Risk
text: The US government is investigating Unknown Aerial Phenomenon (UAP) and discussing the Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Disclosure Act, which could become law. The act would allow the government to seize non-human technologies currently being developed by private corporations, potentially impacting investment markets. Whistleblower David Grusch claims the US government possesses spacecraft reverse-engineered with non-human technology, which could significantly benefit companies such as Lockheed, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, Raytheon, and SpaceX.

title: Will Segment Revenues Aid Northrop's (NOC) Q2 Earnings?
text: Northrop's (NOC) Q2 results are likely to reflect solid sales growth across the majority of its segments. However, higher labor and material costs due to inflation may have impacted the bottom line.

title: Northrop Grumman Stock: A Realistic Approach
text: Shares of Northrop Grumman Corporation soared in 2022 in anticipation of further growth following the war between Ukraine and Russia. This and inflation are not really showing up in the results, which are lagging a bit, notably 2023 margins. Given higher anticipations, modest growth, lower margins this year, and higher net debt, I understand the recent underperformance of Northrop Grumman shares.

title: Raytheon, Northrop Grumman get U.S. defense contract to keep testing hypersonic weapons
text: Weapons developers Raytheon and Northrop Grumman Corp. NOC, +0.80% have been awarded a U.S. Defense Department contract to continue testing and building out ultra-high-speed hypersonic missile systems, executives at both companies said on Monday. The follow-on contract was awarded by the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, or DARPA, to lower risk for those systems and incorporate manufacturing improvements into their design.

title: Raytheon, Northrop Grumman Awarded Contract for Additional Hypersonic Weapon Advancements
text: Additional Flight Tests Offer Opportunity to Further Expand and Validate System Capabilities TUCSON, Ariz. , July 17, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Raytheon, an RTX (NYSE: RTX) business, in partnership with Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC), was awarded a follow-on contract from the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) to reduce risk for future air breathing hypersonic systems.

title: 7 Undervalued Dividend Stocks With Growth Potential for Investors

title: Northrop (NOC) Wins Contract to Aid France's E-2D Hawkeye Jets
text: Northrop (NOC) is set to supply spare parts for E-2D Advanced Hawkeye aircraft to support flight test, following the delivery for three unique configurations of these jets.

title: Northrop Grumman Elects Kathryn Simpson Corporate Vice President and General Counsel; Sheila Cheston to Retire
text: FALLS CHURCH, Va., July 12, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) announces that its board of directors has elected Kathryn Simpson corporate vice president and general counsel, effective October 30, 2023. She will succeed Sheila Cheston, who has announced her intent to retire effective December 1. Simpson will report to Kathy Warden, chair, chief executive officer and president, and oversee the company's legal matters.
